key_points: Apartheid was a system of racial discrimination practiced in South Africa
Nelson Mandela led the struggle against apartheid and later became President of South Africa
A constitution is a set of basic rules and principles according to which a country is governed
The constitution defines the structure and powers of government institutions
A constitution also protects the rights of citizens
The Constituent Assembly was formed in 1946 to draft the Constitution of India
Dr. B.R. Ambedkar was the chairman of the Drafting Committee
The Constitution of India was adopted on 26 November 1949
The Constitution came into effect on 26 January 1950
The Preamble introduces the Constitution and reflects its ideals and philosophy
examples: Chairman of Drafting Committee: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar
President of Constituent Assembly: Dr. Rajendra Prasad
Day Constitution came into force: 26 January 1950
formulas: Sovereign → Independent authority of a country to make its own decisions
Secular → Equal respect for all religions by the state
Republic → Head of the state is elected by the people
Liberty → Freedom of thought, expression and belief
Equality → Equal rights and opportunities for all citizens
Fraternity → Sense of brotherhood and unity among citizens
exam_tips: Remember that the Preamble begins with 'We, the People of India'
Nelson Mandela is an important example of leadership against apartheid
Dr. B.R. Ambedkar played a key role in drafting the Indian Constitution
Republic Day marks the day the Constitution came into force
Justice, Liberty, Equality and Fraternity are key values in the Preamble
